title: Ear Infection Basics
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Ear infections can be caused by bacteria or viruses and can affect the middle ear.
Antibiotics are not always needed; clinician judgment determines whether antibiotics or observation are appropriate.
Patients should seek care for severe symptoms, worsening symptoms, or symptoms that do not improve as expected.
This source supports general education and antibiotic stewardship, not ED-specific disposition decisions.
